info.title: Agent Swarm API
info.description:
Multi-agent orchestration API for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Enables task distribution, agent communication, and service discovery.

MCP tools are documented separately in [MCP.md](./MCP.md).

200.description: Connection result — `{ ok: true, agentName, model }` on success or `{ ok: false, error }` on any failure (missing config, Anthropic API error). Always 200 OK.

post.summary: Ingest content into memory system (async embedding)

Re-embed all memories using the current embedding provider

agentId.description: Re-embed only this agent's memories. Omit for all.

post.summary: List or semantically search memories across all agents (debug/admin)

query.description: Natural-language query. If present, runs semantic search; otherwise lists by recency.

agentId.description: Filter to a single agent. Omit for all.

sourcePath.description: Substring match against sourcePath (case-insensitive). Useful for file_index memories.

delete.summary: Delete a single memory by ID (debug/admin)

post.summary: Submit RatingEvents to update memory usefulness posteriors

referencesSource.description: Optional external source ID this memory references. Free-form string, convention "<source>:<identifier>" (e.g. "github:owner/repo#N", "linear:KEY-N", "customer:<slug>", "slack:<channel>:<ts>", "agentmail:<thread-id>"). Pick any prefix that fits — no closed enum. When present, an edge from this memory to the external source is created/updated.

200.description: Ratings applied; per-event rejections returned in body

400.description: Validation error or explicit-self R6 spam-guard rejection

get.summary: Redirect to Atlassian OAuth consent screen
Handle Jira OAuth callback (resolves cloudId via accessible-resources)

500.description: Token exchange or accessible-resources fetch failed

get.summary: Jira connection status, cloudId/siteUrl, token expiry, expected webhook URL, scope/token-config flags

post.summary: Force a Jira OAuth token refresh and return the updated status payload. Useful when an agent observes an expired token via tracker-status / db-query and wants to recover without restarting the server or re-running 3LO.

200.description: Token refreshed; returns same shape as /status

409.description: Jira not connected (no refresh token stored)

500.description: Refresh failed (e.g. revoked grant, network error)

post.summary: Receive Jira webhook events (URL-token authenticated). Phase 2 stub — Phase 3 fills in dispatch.
Register a Jira dynamic webhook (admin only)

503.description: Jira not connected or JIRA_WEBHOOK_TOKEN missing

delete.summary: Delete a Jira dynamic webhook (admin only)
Fully disconnect Jira: delete all webhooks, drop tokens, clear metadata

Handle Linear OAuth callback
Linear connection status, token expiry, workspace info, expected webhook URL

post.summary: Force a Linear OAuth token refresh and return the updated status payload. Useful when an agent observes an expired token and wants to recover without restarting the server or re-running OAuth.

200.description: Token refreshed; returns same shape as /status

409.description: Linear not connected (no refresh token stored)

post.summary: Handle Linear webhook events (signature-verified)

delete.summary: Fully disconnect Linear: revoke OAuth grant + drop tokens

post.summary: Handle GitHub webhook events
Handle GitLab webhook events
Handle AgentMail webhook events
Fire a run-scoped event signal
post.description: Emits an event onto the workflow event bus with `_runId` injected. Used by wait nodes in `event` mode with `scope: 'run'`. The body's `name` is the bus event name; `payload` is forwarded as-is plus `_runId`.

post.summary: Fire a global workflow event signal
post.description: Emits an event onto the workflow event bus. Wait-states with `scope: 'global'` may match. Run-scoped waits will NOT match this broadcast unless the payload carries a matching `workflowRunId`.

get.summary: List all workflows

post.summary: Create a new workflow

id.description: Unique node identifier, used in 'next' and 'inputs' mappings

config.description: Executor-specific config. For agent-task: { template, outputSchema?, agentId?, tags?, priority?, dir?, vcsRepo?, model? }. Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

next.description: Next node(s): string for simple chaining, string[] for fan-out to parallel nodes, or record for port-based routing ({pass: 'a', fail: 'b'})

inputs.description: REQUIRED for cross-node data access. Maps local names to context paths. Without this, upstream step outputs are NOT available for interpolation — only 'trigger' and 'input' are. Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

onNodeFailure.description: Behavior when a node's task fails or is cancelled. 'fail' (default): mark the entire run as failed. 'continue': treat the failed node as completed with error output and proceed — downstream convergence nodes receive '[FAILED: reason]' and can handle partial results.

get.summary: Get a workflow by ID

Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

triggerSchema.description: Optional JSON-Schema describing the expected trigger payload shape. Pass an object to set/replace; pass null to clear; omit to leave unchanged. Validator subset: type, required, properties, enum, const, items. Other JSON-Schema keywords are silently ignored.

patch.summary: Patch a single node in a workflow definition

config.description: Executor-specific config. For agent-task: { template, outputSchema?, agentId?, tags?, priority?, dir?, vcsRepo?, model? }. Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

post.summary: Trigger a workflow execution

201.description: Workflow run started (or skipped if cooldown active)

post.summary: Validate a payload against the workflow's triggerSchema (no run)

200.description: Payload matches the workflow's triggerSchema (or workflow has none)

400.description: Payload failed validation; body matches the TriggerSchemaError contract
